name: Artichoke Croustades
recipeIngredient: 18 slices Soft white bread; crusts removed
6 ounces Marinated artichoke hearts; drained and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Roll each bread slice flat with a rolling pin and trim it to form a 2 1/2-inch square.
2. Brush both sides of the squares lightly with the butter.
3. Fit the squares into gem muffin tins, pressing the bread against the sides of the tins.
4. Bake the croustades in the middle of a preheated 350°F oven for 12-14 minutes, or until the edges are pale golden.
5. These may be made up to 2 days in advance and kept in an airtight container.
6. In a bowl, stir together the artichoke hearts, mayonnaise, 1 tablespoon of scallion, 1/4 cup of Parmesan, salt, and pepper to taste.
7. Divide the mixture by heaping teaspoons among the croustades and sprinkle the remaining Parmesan on top.
8. Broil the croustades under a preheated broiler about 6 inches from the heat for 1 minute, or until the filling is bubbling.
9. Garnish the croustades with the scallion green and arrange 3 of them on each of 6 small plates.
